blurb:
Someone found Antigua and her siblings outside without their mom and got them to Mostly Mutts. They spent time in a foster home, then moved into the shelter's Kitty Room, where Antigua has been busy doing what two-and-a-half-month-old kittens do: playing hard and then climbing into a lap the second play time ends.

She's about two pounds right now and still growing. The volunteers say she splits her time pretty evenly between chasing toys and looking for someone to snuggle with, which sounds like a pretty solid schedule honestly. She's been great with her siblings and the other kittens in the shelter, so if you've got a cat at home who might enjoy a young companion, she's worth considering.

A few practical things: she's had her first round of kitten shots but will need the rest completed as she grows, and she's microchipped. She's not yet spayed due to her size, but it's required and will be done before she goes home. Gentle kids are welcome. Dog compatibility is unknown, so a meet and greet would be the move if you've got one.

Antigua is at the Mostly Mutts shelter in the Kitty Room. Stop by or check their website to set up a visit.
shelter_address: 1000 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Suite 450, Kennesaw, GA 30144
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current hours and adoption appointment availability
scoring_notes:
• Classic kitten profile — playful, snuggly, cat-friendly, and kid-tolerant — delivers reliable newsletter click-and-share appeal
• Orphaned-outside origin adds a soft emotional hook but is not unusual enough to stand alone as a compelling narrative
• Appears to have arrived recently with siblings still present, indicating low shelter time and limited urgency relative to other candidates
